notes: Interesting textile concept but canvas oven mitts exist, hard to differentiate from existing brands
Drip pans for grills are commodity items, too much competition at low price points
Offal preparation is too small a market in US, specialty cutting boards exist
Chef knife market is brutally competitive, hard to differentiate without major capital
Wooden serving boards are everywhere, no meaningful differentiation from existing options
Market limited to mate drinkers, twist to general drinkware cleaning is weak
Knife storage is commodity, magnetic strips and blocks exist in every price range
Salsa criolla is too unknown in US, hard to create demand for unknown condiment
Knife rests exist, hard to build brand around such simple item
Fernet culture doesn't exist in US outside niche communities, general cocktail measure already covered
Bread baskets and pastry trays are commodity items, no gap
Carbonada is unknown in US, seasonal serving dishes exist
Pizza rocker blades exist for thick pizzas, hard to differentiate
Meat mallets are commodity kitchen items, hard to build premium brand
Wine aerators and decanters are everywhere, saturated market
